content:
Consider the objective function $Z=x-y$ subject to the constraints

$$ \begin{aligned} & x+2 y \leq 10 \\ & x+y \geq 2 \\ & x \geq 0, y \geq 0 \end{aligned} $$

What is the minimum value of $Z$ subject to the above constraints?
explanation:
The constraints: $\left\{\begin{array}{l}x+2 y \leq 10 \\ x+y \geq 2 \\ x \geq 0 \\ y \geq 0\end{array}\right.$

Identify the corner points (vertices) of the feasible region.

Determine the minimum value of $Z$.

First, convert the inequalities to equations to find the boundary lines.

$$ \begin{gathered} x+2 y=10 \\ x+y=2 \end{gathered} $$

Intersection Points with Axes

1.For $x+2 y=10$ :

When $x=0: 2 y=10 \Rightarrow y=5$ (Point: $(0,5)$ )

When $y=0: x=10$ (Point: $(10,0)$ )

    2.For $x+y=2$ :

    When $x=0: y=2$ (Point: $(0,2)$ )

    When $y=0: x=2$ (Point: $(2,0)$ )

    $1.(0,5)$$2.\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(2,0)$

    $$ \begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline(x, y) & Z=x-y \\ (0,5) & Z=0-5=-5 \\ \hline(2,0) & Z=2-0=2 \\ \hline \end{array} $$
